description:
You are a Linux systems engineer who has just received an insecure sample Apache HTTP Server
    configuration located in:

        /home/user/service_configs/httpd.conf.orig

    Your goal is to harden that configuration and document every single change you make.

    STEP-BY-STEP OBJECTIVES
    1. Create the directory     /home/user/service_configs/backup        (if it does not already exist).
    2. Copy the original file    /home/user/service_configs/httpd.conf.orig   into that backup directory
       using the exact name      httpd.conf.orig.bak
       so the original can always be restored.
    3. Produce a NEW hardened configuration file named
          /home/user/service_configs/httpd_hardened.conf
       by starting from the original file and making the six hardening changes listed below.
       Make no other textual changes—keep comments and blank lines exactly as they were.

       REQUIRED HARDENING CHANGES
       a.  ServerTokens    must be set to  Prod
       b.  ServerSignature must be set to  Off
       c.  Options         must remove “Indexes” and explicitly keep “FollowSymLinks”,
                           expressed exactly as “Options -Indexes +FollowSymLinks”
       d.  AllowOverride   must be set to  None
       e.  SSLProtocol     must be narrowed to the two protocols “TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3”
       f.  Timeout         must be lowered to 60

    4. For audit purposes create the CSV log file

          /home/user/service_configs/hardening.log

       with the following SPECIFIC format so that automated tests can validate it:

       · The very first line is the header:
             Setting,OldValue,NewValue
       · One subsequent line for **each** of the six required changes
         in exactly the order shown above.
       · Every line ends with a newline character (“\n”).
       · Do NOT place extra spaces around the commas.
       · Example of what a single line should look like:
             ServerTokens,Full,Prod

       When you are done, the file must contain exactly 7 lines (1 header + 6 change rows).

    5. Verify that:
       • /home/user/service_configs/httpd_hardened.conf exists  
       • /home/user/service_configs/hardening.log exists  
       • The hardened configuration reflects all six required changes and only those changes.  
       • The CSV log format is correct as described above.

    Once you are satisfied that everything matches these requirements, the task is complete.
truth:
<!--
      Pre-existing state before the agent starts.
    -->
    <preExisting>
        <directory path="/home/user/service_configs" />
        <file path="/home/user/service_configs/httpd.conf.orig"><![CDATA[
# Example Apache HTTPD Configuration
ServerRoot "/usr/local/apache2"
Listen 80
LoadModule mpm_event_module modules/mod_mpm_event.so
User daemon
Group daemon
ServerTokens Full
ServerSignature On
Options Indexes FollowSymLinks
AllowOverride All
SSLProtocol all -SSLv2 -SSLv3 -TLSv1
Timeout 300
]]></file>
    </preExisting>

    <!--
      Expected final state for automated verification.
    -->
    <expectedFinal>
        <directory path="/home/user/service_configs/backup" />

        <file path="/home/user/service_configs/backup/httpd.conf.orig.bak"
              checksum="sha256:PLACEHOLDER_SHA_OF_ORIGINAL_FILE" />

        <file path="/home/user/service_configs/httpd_hardened.conf"><![CDATA[
# Example Apache HTTPD Configuration
ServerRoot "/usr/local/apache2"
Listen 80
LoadModule mpm_event_module modules/mod_mpm_event.so
User daemon
Group daemon
ServerTokens Prod
ServerSignature Off
Options -Indexes +FollowSymLinks
AllowOverride None
SSLProtocol TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3
Timeout 60
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/service_configs/hardening.log"><![CDATA[
Setting,OldValue,NewValue
ServerTokens,Full,Prod
ServerSignature,On,Off
Options,Indexes FollowSymLinks,-Indexes +FollowSymLinks
AllowOverride,All,None
SSLProtocol,all -SSLv2 -SSLv3 -TLSv1,TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3
Timeout,300,60
]]></file>
    </expectedFinal>
